title: CHILD WELFARE & DEVELOPMENTAL PSYCHOLOGY BRIEF
subtitle: Research-Based Arguments for L.D.W.'s Best Interests

categories.relevance: L.D.W. age 2-3 is in CRITICAL attachment formation window

points.point: Bowlby Attachment Theory: Secure attachment to BOTH parents is essential for healthy development
Ainsworth Strange Situation: Children who lose access to attachment figure show protest → despair → detachment
Ages 1-3 are the primary attachment window — disruption during this period causes lasting harm
Overnight stays with non-custodial parent strengthen secure attachment
points.source: Warshak (2014) Social Science and Parenting Plans

points.point: Complete severance of parent-child contact is the MOST harmful outcome — worse than conflict exposure

categories.relevance: L.D.W. born Nov 9, 2022 — currently in critical development period

points.point: Language explosion (ages 2-3): Children learn 200+ new words — needs BOTH parents' verbal input
Social-emotional development: Learns trust, empathy, self-regulation through parent interactions
Gender identity formation begins: Father-child relationship is critical for healthy identity development
points.source: Lamb (2010) The Role of the Father

points.point: Cognitive development: Object permanence → understanding that absent parent still exists and loves them
Play-based learning: Different parent styles (rough-and-tumble with fathers) serve distinct developmental functions

categories.category: Impact of Parental Alienation on Toddlers
categories.relevance: Emily's pattern of excluding Andrew from L.D.W.'s life

points.point: Children who lose contact with a parent show increased anxiety, depression, and behavioral problems
points.source: Bernet (2010) Parental Alienation — Science and Law

points.point: Toddlers cannot articulate their distress — manifests as sleep disruption, regression, aggression
points.source: APA Task Force on Children & Divorce

points.point: Long-term effects: Children alienated from a parent have higher rates of substance abuse and relationship failure
points.source: Baker (2007) Adult Children of Parental Alienation

points.point: The alienating parent's behavior — not the rejected parent's — is the primary risk factor
Courts increasingly recognize alienation as a form of child abuse

categories.relevance: Andrew's role as engaged father documented by 26 strengths (tool #157)

points.point: Father involvement predicts better academic performance, social competence, and psychological well-being
Children with involved fathers have 33% fewer behavioral problems
Father absence is linked to 5x higher poverty rate, 2x higher dropout rate
Michigan law creates PRESUMPTION of parenting time — MCL 722.27a(1)
Best Interest Factor (j): Willingness to facilitate close relationship — Emily's behavior is dispositive

categories.relevance: Specific arguments to present to new judge after McNeill removal

points.point: ARGUMENT 1: Complete parenting time suspension violates L.D.W.'s fundamental right to both parents
ARGUMENT 2: No evidence that Andrew poses any risk — suspension based solely on Emily's unverified allegations
points.authority: MCL 722.27a(7) — burden of proof on party seeking restriction

points.point: ARGUMENT 3: L.D.W. is in critical developmental window — every day of separation causes measurable harm
ARGUMENT 4: Emily's alienating behavior is itself grounds for custody modification
ARGUMENT 5: Gradual reunification protocol should begin IMMEDIATELY — delay compounds harm
ARGUMENT 6: GAL appointment will confirm Andrew's fitness and Emily's alienation
